author: PC Gamer
text:
Former Valve writer Chet Faliszek has no interest in writing Half-Life 3: 'I don't want to touch that with a 10-foot pole'
"Or even a grav gun separating me from that 10-foot pole."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Lego racing game 2K Drive will be delisted next week, but online multiplayer won't shut down until next year
It released just three years ago.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Forza Horizon 6 has hit a higher peak player count than Forza Horizon 5 and it's not even out yet
Meching money.

author: PC Gamer
text:
It's time for Garfield's official Magic: The Gathering crossover now
No, not Richard Garfield. The other one.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Star Citizen's latest alpha update brings a 'full reset' to its universe, as Cloud Imperium Games promises to crack down on player exploits and duplication issues
Players keep their blueprints and ships bought with in-game currency, but that's it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Civilization 7's imminent update will add one of history's most famous conquerors for free, in Firaxis' latest bid to coax you back to its controversial sequel
Tests of Time has one last surprise in store.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This indie action RPG looks like Minecraft but plays like Dark Messiah, letting you boot enemies off ledges and body slam them into walls of spikes
The Adventures of Sir Kicksalot is out now

author: PC Gamer
text:
Scott Miller, founder of Apogee, has over 1,000 hours in World of Warcraft and always plays a warrior in any RPG: 'I like to get right in their face and pound away'
The shareware pioneer guides us through the games on his PC.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Demigod review (2009)
One of the gods has fallen. Demigod is your chance to replace him. The problem? The other seven applicants.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Debate: What truly is the easiest character to cosplay?
Sometimes the prep and planning just doesn't cut it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth's director weighs in on the real-time versus turn-based debate, saying that younger players 'increasingly favour more real-time experiences in games'
But Hamaguchi still reckons that turn-based games are "deeply universal."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Directive 8020's new Turning Points system transforms it from a simple sci-fi survival horror into a compelling, gory puzzle
Cheat fate.
